_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.shape: A yield curve plots Treasury yields across different maturities, typically from 1 month to 30 years. An upward-sloping curve indicates higher yields for longer maturities, suggesting normal economic conditions. Currently, the 10-year Treasury yield is 4.55% with the 10Y-2Y spread at 0.35 points, indicating a positive slope. This contrasts with the period from 2022 to 2024 when the curve was inverted, with the 2s10s spread below zero.
notes.signal: The 10-year minus 2-year (2s10s) spread is a widely used recession indicator; an inversion (spread below zero) has historically preceded US recessions by 6-18 months. The deepest inversion on record was 0.0 points, while the current spread of 0.35 points shows that the curve has re-steepened above zero following a prolonged inversion during 2022-2024. The New York Fed's recession probability stands at 31%, consistent with moderate risk. However, yield curve un-inversions often occur shortly before recessions begin, which warrants a cautious interpretation.
notes.now: The yield curve's positive 2s10s spread suggests a return to a normal upward slope after a period of inversion, while the 31% recession probability indicates moderate economic uncertainty. Together, these factors imply the market is currently in a phase of relative normalization following an earlier signal of stress, without indicating imminent recession timing.
